title: The West Built the Cheap China System. Now It Wants Poor People to Pay More.
source_location: Draft TWIS article text reviewed during controlled Evidence Pack v1 workflow test.
notes: Article-draft source used as a controlled TWIS article/sandbox pack. This is not a full external-source pack.

title: EU to impose 3 euro duty on e-commerce parcels from July 2026
source_location: Reuters report on EU finance ministers agreeing a temporary 3 euro duty on low-value e-commerce parcels from July 1, 2026.
notes: First external source added to the second controlled TWIS article/sandbox pack. Supports the low-value parcel duty component only.

title: Commission announces actions for safe and sustainable e-commerce imports
notes: Primary EU source. Supports the official EU framing on low-value consignments, the 150 euro threshold, customs reform, market fairness, compliance pressure, and environmental concerns. Does not itself support the later Reuters-specific 3 euro July 2026 duty.

title: Revealed: how UK's poor paid price of cheapflation in cost of living crisis
source_location: Guardian report on Institute for Fiscal Studies research into cheapflation and grocery-price impact on poorer households.
notes: Consumer-impact source. Supports the general claim that price rises on cheaper goods can hit poorer households harder. It does not prove the specific EU parcel duty impact or Chinese platform consumer behaviour.
